title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Runtime Contract: Pipeline Response Order
summary: A server dispatches the next request before consuming the current body, writes responses out of order, reuses connections after framing errors, and waits forever for idle peers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pipeline response order increment in persistent-connection request and response state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve request-response ordering across pipelined HTTP/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order.
Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pipeline Response Order · Theory Pipeline Response Order
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8822 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pipeline Response Order contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Pipeline Response Order · Theory Pipeline Response Order.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve request-response ordering across pipelined http/1.1 messages even when application work finishes out of order. For Predict Pipeline Response Order · Theory Pipeline Response Order, keep Pipeline Response Order connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
